_note: In/out LOCKED from the SHRM timestamped transcript. captions[] = verbatim spoken text (Whisper can re-split for word-level). overlays[] = animated stat callouts (big number pops on-brand when spoken); startSec/endSec are ABSOLUTE source seconds within the clip's in/out. Host: Monique Akanbi. Guest: Ginny Crisp.

clips.locatorQuote: PBMs are allowed to have contracts where you're not paying that pass-through amount

captions.text: PBMs are allowed to have contracts
where you're not paying that pass-through
amount, meaning that they could pay
the pharmacy one price for that
GLP-1 prescription, but then turn around
and invoice you a very different
amount, which is gonna be higher.
And they keep that difference. And
in our industry, that's called spread
pricing, which costs you more money
than you would need to pay
on those medications and not just
for GLP-1s, but for everything. So
I think the first question is,

if it's because they can't afford

that's a different situation.

_note_v2: *v2 entries (Jul 2026) are ELEVATED SECOND VERSIONS of the same moments — identical in/out, captions, and cutaways, plus the v2 treatment (elevate: punch-in editing rhythm on caption boundaries, Accent-tinted emphasis words in the karaoke track, animated end card). The original clip entries are untouched and still render the verified v1 output. Batch-render: node render-with-extract.mjs v2. Optional music bed: add music:{src,volume} to a v2 clip once a licensed track exists at remotion_starter/public/ (render script skips it if the file is missing).
